rag_rules: Preserve scope correctness when fusing: type-level contract content belongs on the component type, while implementation-level bindings belong on the implementation.
Insert implementation-scope AGREE content only in a valid implementation annex and preserve surrounding subcomponents, connections, and properties.
Do not move references across scopes during fusion; content in a type scope must not depend on subcomponent ports unless that visibility is explicitly represented.
generic_rules: Make the smallest structural edit that attaches the generated annex to the intended owner without changing unrelated architecture.
Preserve existing declarations, ordering, and formatting as much as possible when adding or updating an annex block.
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: AGREE content is enclosed in an `annex agree {** ... **};` block.
Implementation-scope bindings should remain in implementation scope; guarantees should not be fused into implementation scope.
anti_patterns: Do not insert contract content under the wrong owner or switch between component type and implementation scopes during fusion.
Do not introduce references to AADL properties or units as if they were AGREE variables while merging annex content.
irrelevant_topics: Requirement decision logic and clause selection details are not part of model fusion.
Type-mismatch repair tactics are validator-oriented and should not drive insertion behavior unless diagnostics require them.
